column1: What is AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model)?
column2: An open source framework optimized for building and deploying serverless applications on AWS by organizing related components and resources in a single stack

column1: What does the events property do in SAM templates?
column2: Allows you to assign Lambda functions to an event source

column1: What are the main advantages of AWS CDK (Cloud Development Kit)?
column2: Lets you program infrastructure-as-code with traditional programming languages and includes abstractions/helper functions that encapsulate complex infrastructure into single commands

column1: What are cloud sandboxes in AWS Amplify?
column2: Feature that lets developers deploy an alternate, personal version of the app's backend infrastructure for use while running the front end locally

column1: What does the cdk synth command do?
column2: Attempts to generate CloudFormation from CDK code to validate there are no compile/transpile time errors before deployment

column1: What can you create with just a few lines in a SAM template?
column2: API Gateway routes backed by Lambda functions that access DynamoDB data

column1: What CloudFormation feature enables SAM syntax to work?
column2: Transform feature - allows sending all or part of a CloudFormation template to an external Lambda for processing, enabling non-native syntax support

column1: How can you grant a Lambda function permissions to access DynamoDB in SAM?
column2: Add DynamoDB table as a resource and configure a managed policy (not environment variables alone or inline policies)

column1: What does SAM NOT assist with for API Gateway?
column2: Domain configuration (but it simplifies routes, Lambda integration, and DynamoDB access)

column1: What makes AWS Amplify suitable for full-stack serverless applications?
column2: Combines serverless backend (API Gateway REST APIs with TypeScript), front-end build/deploy tools (React, VueJS), and authentication (Cognito) in a single package

column1: Does SAM help with front-end deployment or user authentication?
column2: No, SAM handles REST API and infrastructure but not front end or authentication

column1: Does AWS CDK help build and deploy web applications?
column2: No, CDK handles infrastructure but doesn't help build and deploy web applications

column1: Is Elastic Beanstalk serverless by default?
column2: No, Beanstalk apps are not serverless by default and it doesn't make adding authentication as simple as Amplify

column1: What is the best practice for granting Lambda permissions in SAM - inline or managed policies?
column2: Managed policies are more flexible and maintainable than inline policies

column1: Should you create resources like DynamoDB tables manually when using SAM?
column2: No, creating tables manually breaks the automation SAM provides - add them as resources in the template
